text: Which anime follows Naruto Uzumaki as he dreams of becoming Hokage?
explanation: Naruto follows a young ninja who wants to earn recognition and become Hokage.

text: In One Piece, what is Monkey D. Luffy searching for?
explanation: Luffy's journey is centered on finding the legendary treasure known as the One Piece.

text: Which anime features Goku and the Dragon Balls?
explanation: Dragon Ball follows Goku and adventures involving the wish-granting Dragon Balls.

text: Which anime centers on Tanjiro Kamado fighting demons?
explanation: Demon Slayer follows Tanjiro after his family is attacked by demons.

text: In Death Note, what object gives Light Yagami the power to kill by writing names?
explanation: The Death Note is a supernatural notebook that kills people whose names are written in it.

text: Which anime is set in a world where humanity hides behind giant walls from Titans?
explanation: Attack on Titan begins with humanity living inside walls to survive the threat of Titans.

text: In Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood, what are Edward and Alphonse Elric trying to restore?
explanation: The Elric brothers seek a way to recover what they lost after a failed human transmutation.

text: Which anime features Ash Ketchum and Pikachu?
explanation: Pokémon follows trainers such as Ash Ketchum and his partner Pikachu.

text: Which anime is about magical cards captured by Sakura Kinomoto?
explanation: Cardcaptor Sakura follows Sakura as she collects magical Clow Cards.

text: Which anime features the Survey Corps?
explanation: The Survey Corps is the military branch that ventures beyond the walls in Attack on Titan.

text: In Bleach, what kind of being is Ichigo Kurosaki closely associated with becoming?
explanation: Ichigo gains Soul Reaper powers and protects humans from dangerous spirits.

text: Which anime features the Straw Hat Pirates?
explanation: The Straw Hat Pirates are Luffy's crew in One Piece.

text: In My Hero Academia, what are superpowers commonly called?
explanation: In My Hero Academia, most people are born with special abilities known as Quirks.

text: Which anime follows Izuku Midoriya at U.A. High School?
explanation: Izuku Midoriya attends U.A. High School to train as a professional hero.

text: In Jujutsu Kaisen, what is Yuji Itadori connected to after eating a cursed object?
explanation: Yuji becomes the vessel of Ryomen Sukuna after swallowing one of Sukuna's fingers.

text: Which anime features cursed spirits and jujutsu sorcerers?
explanation: Jujutsu Kaisen centers on sorcerers who fight curses born from negative human emotions.

text: In Chainsaw Man, what kind of devil is Pochita?
explanation: Pochita is the Chainsaw Devil and becomes central to Denji's transformation.

text: Which anime features a hero named Saitama who defeats enemies with one punch?
explanation: One-Punch Man follows Saitama, a hero so strong he often wins with a single punch.

text: Which anime follows Shigeo Kageyama, a powerful psychic nicknamed Mob?
answers: The Disastrous Life of Saiki K.
explanation: Mob Psycho 100 follows Mob, a quiet boy with extraordinary psychic abilities.

text: In Hunter x Hunter, what energy system do skilled fighters learn to use?
explanation: Nen is the core aura-based power system in Hunter x Hunter.

text: Which anime features Gon Freecss searching for his father?
explanation: Gon becomes a Hunter partly to find his father, Ging Freecss.

text: Which anime features the alchemy rule known as equivalent exchange?
explanation: Equivalent exchange is a central principle of alchemy in Fullmetal Alchemist.

text: Which anime follows a volleyball team from Karasuno High?
explanation: Haikyu!! centers on Karasuno High School's volleyball team.

text: Which anime focuses on a high-stakes soccer training program to create the world's best striker?
explanation: Blue Lock is built around an intense program designed to produce an elite striker.

text: Which anime is about basketball and the Generation of Miracles?
explanation: Kuroko's Basketball features the legendary middle-school team known as the Generation of Miracles.

text: Which anime follows Hanamichi Sakuragi as he joins a basketball team?
explanation: Slam Dunk follows Sakuragi's growth after joining Shohoku's basketball team.

text: Which anime is centered on competitive figure skating and Yuri Katsuki?
explanation: Yuri!!! on Ice follows figure skater Yuri Katsuki and coach Victor Nikiforov.

text: Which anime features a group of Scouts fighting Titans?
explanation: The Scout Regiment fights Titans and investigates the world beyond the walls.

text: In Tokyo Ghoul, what does Ken Kaneki become after a transplant?
explanation: Kaneki becomes part-ghoul after receiving organs from a ghoul.

text: Which anime features a coffee shop called Anteiku?
explanation: Anteiku is an important meeting place for ghouls in Tokyo Ghoul.

text: In Parasyte, what is the name often used for the parasite in Shinichi's right hand?
explanation: Migi is the parasite that takes over Shinichi's right hand in Parasyte.

text: Which anime involves a device called the Sibyl System?

text: Which anime follows Rintaro Okabe and experiments with time travel?

text: Which anime features a time-looping protagonist named Subaru Natsuki?

text: Which anime features a notebook carried by a shinigami named Ryuk?

text: Which anime features Edward Elric with a metal arm and leg?

text: Which anime features a pirate crew sailing the Grand Line?

text: In Naruto, what is the name of the Nine-Tails sealed inside Naruto?

text: Which anime features the Hidden Leaf Village?

text: In Demon Slayer, what breathing style does Tanjiro first learn formally?

text: Which anime features Nezuko Kamado?

text: Which anime features a family using false identities, including Loid, Yor, and Anya?

text: In Spy x Family, what special ability does Anya have?

text: Which anime features Kaguya Shinomiya and Miyuki Shirogane trying to make the other confess?

explanation: Rimuru is reincarnated as a slime and builds a nation of monsters.

text: Which anime is famous for the phrase “Believe in the me that believes in you”?

text: Which long-running franchise helped define the real robot mecha genre?

text: Which anime follows jazz-loving students Kaoru and Sentaro in the 1960s?
explanation: Kids on the Slope blends coming-of-age drama with jazz music.

text: Which anime follows Koyuki joining the band BECK?
explanation: Beck follows Koyuki's growth through rock music and friendship.

text: Which anime follows a cursed girl named Rika and sorcerer Yuta Okkotsu in its prequel film?
explanation: Jujutsu Kaisen 0 focuses on Yuta Okkotsu and Rika before the main series.

text: Which anime film adapts the Mugen Train arc?
explanation: Mugen Train continues Tanjiro's story with Rengoku and the Flame Hashira.

text: Which Dragon Ball film introduced Broly into the main modern continuity?
answers: Dragon Ball Z: Battle of Gods
explanation: Dragon Ball Super: Broly reintroduced Broly in the Dragon Ball Super era.

text: Which One Piece film features the singer Uta?
explanation: One Piece Film: Red centers heavily on Uta, a world-famous singer.

text: Which anime features Phos, a gem-like protagonist?
explanation: Phos is the central Lustrous character whose identity changes throughout the story.

text: Which anime features Tanjiro's black Nichirin sword?
explanation: Nichirin swords are used by Demon Slayers, and Tanjiro's blade turns black.

text: In Bleach, what is the name for a Soul Reaper's sword?
explanation: A Zanpakuto is the signature weapon of Soul Reapers in Bleach.

text: In Naruto, what energy is used to perform many ninja techniques?
explanation: Chakra is the energy source used for jutsu in Naruto.

text: In Bleach, what spiritual pressure is commonly called?
explanation: Reiatsu refers to spiritual pressure in Bleach.

text: In One Piece, what power system includes Observation, Armament, and Conqueror's forms?
explanation: Haki is a major power system in One Piece with several forms.

text: Which anime follows Eren Yeager after Titans destroy his hometown?
explanation: Attack on Titan begins with Eren witnessing the fall of his home and joining the fight against Titans.

text: In Naruto, which team originally includes Naruto, Sasuke, Sakura, and Kakashi?
explanation: Team 7 is Naruto's original squad under Kakashi Hatake.

text: In One Piece, what is Zoro's dream?
answers: To become the world's greatest swordsman
correctAnswer: To become the world's greatest swordsman
explanation: Roronoa Zoro aims to become the greatest swordsman in the world.

text: In Bleach, what is Rukia Kuchiki at the start of the story?
explanation: Rukia is a Soul Reaper whose meeting with Ichigo changes his life.

text: Which anime features a school for heroes called U.A. High?
explanation: U.A. High is the elite hero-training school in My Hero Academia.

text: In Demon Slayer, which Hashira uses Flame Breathing?
explanation: Kyojuro Rengoku is the Flame Hashira in Demon Slayer.

text: In Jujutsu Kaisen, who is known for the Limitless technique and Six Eyes?
explanation: Gojo is famous for the Limitless cursed technique and the Six Eyes.

text: Which anime features Denji merging with Pochita?
explanation: Denji becomes Chainsaw Man after merging with Pochita.

text: In Hunter x Hunter, what is Killua Zoldyck's family known for?
explanation: The Zoldyck family is a famous family of assassins.

text: Which Hunter x Hunter arc features the Chimera Ants?
explanation: The Chimera Ant arc is one of Hunter x Hunter's most famous and intense story arcs.

text: Which anime features a yellow electric mouse named Pikachu?
explanation: Pikachu is the iconic partner Pokémon of Ash Ketchum.

text: Which anime features digital monsters partnered with children?
explanation: Digimon Adventure follows children partnered with digital monsters in the Digital World.

text: Which anime franchise features duelists using cards and monsters?
explanation: Yu-Gi-Oh! centers on card duels involving monsters, spells, and traps.

text: Which anime is about spinning tops called Beyblades?
explanation: Beyblade centers on competitive battles using customized spinning tops.

text: Which anime features the Elric brothers searching for the Philosopher's Stone?
explanation: Edward and Alphonse search for the Philosopher's Stone to restore their bodies.

text: Which Fullmetal Alchemist character is known as the Flame Alchemist?
explanation: Roy Mustang is known as the Flame Alchemist because of his fire-based alchemy.

text: Which anime features the Black Bulls magic knight squad?
explanation: The Black Bulls are Asta's magic knight squad in Black Clover.

text: Which anime follows Asta, a boy born without magic?
explanation: Asta dreams of becoming Wizard King despite being born without magic.

text: Which anime features a wizard guild named Fairy Tail?
explanation: Fairy Tail follows the adventures of a magical guild of the same name.

text: Which anime features Natsu Dragneel and Happy?
explanation: Natsu and his flying cat companion Happy are central characters in Fairy Tail.

text: Which anime features the Seven Deadly Sins as a group of knights?
explanation: The anime follows a legendary group of knights named after the Seven Deadly Sins.

text: Which anime follows Alibaba, Aladdin, and Morgiana?
explanation: Magi draws inspiration from Arabian Nights-style adventure and follows Aladdin, Alibaba, and Morgiana.

text: Which anime features dungeon captures and Djinn Equip?
explanation: Magi includes magical dungeons and powers granted by Djinn.

text: Which anime features a group called Night Raid fighting a corrupt empire?
explanation: Akame ga Kill! follows Night Raid as they oppose a brutal empire.

text: Which anime follows female warriors known as Claymores?
explanation: Claymore centers on hybrid warriors who fight monsters called Yoma.

text: Which anime features a demon butler named Sebastian Michaelis?
explanation: Sebastian Michaelis serves Ciel Phantomhive as a demon butler.

text: Which anime follows Allen Walker and the Black Order?
explanation: D.Gray-man follows exorcists of the Black Order fighting Akuma.

text: Which anime features a book that can kill people by name?
explanation: The Death Note kills people whose names are written inside while picturing their face.

text: Which anime follows Dr. Kenzo Tenma after he saves Johan Liebert?
explanation: Monster follows Tenma as he confronts the consequences of saving Johan Liebert.

text: Which anime features the mysterious attacker known as Lil' Slugger?
explanation: Paranoia Agent revolves around social anxiety and attacks by Lil' Slugger.

text: Which anime is centered on high-stakes gambling at Hyakkaou Private Academy?
explanation: Kakegurui follows students whose school hierarchy is shaped by gambling.

text: Which anime follows Kaiji Itou through extreme gambling and debt games?
explanation: Kaiji is known for tense gambling scenarios and psychological pressure.

text: Which anime focuses on mahjong genius Shigeru Akagi?
explanation: Akagi follows a legendary mahjong player in high-stakes matches.

text: Which anime mixes baseball strategy with gambling psychology through Tokuchi Toua?
explanation: One Outs turns baseball into a psychological battle centered on pitcher Tokuchi Toua.

text: Which anime follows Eijun Sawamura pursuing baseball at Seidou High?
explanation: Ace of Diamond follows Sawamura as he develops as a pitcher at Seidou High.

text: Which anime follows Goro Honda's life through baseball across many years?
explanation: Major tracks Goro's baseball journey from childhood into professional play.

text: Which anime is a boxing classic about Ippo Makunouchi?
explanation: Hajime no Ippo follows Ippo's growth from bullied student to boxer.

text: Which anime reimagines boxing through gear-enhanced fights called Megalobox?
explanation: Megalo Box features boxing-like matches enhanced by mechanical gear.

text: Which classic boxing anime follows Joe Yabuki?
explanation: Ashita no Joe is a foundational boxing anime centered on Joe Yabuki.

text: Which anime follows martial artist Baki Hanma?
explanation: Baki follows intense martial arts battles centered on Baki Hanma.
